codeReview.summary: The main Store/Repo conversion seam matches the feature intent and the grouped store_sensitive_* tests cover the approved CRUD APIs, but the implementation still leaves create_return_id on the public Crud surface without any sensitive-model guard or acceptance coverage. That leaves a Store API reachable by #[derive(Store, Sensitive)] callers outside the milestone’s explicit consistent-contract matrix.

issues.description: Crud::create_return_id remains publicly available for all Store models by delegating to Repo::<Self>::create_return_id(self.clone()).await, yet the mission architecture notes explicitly excluded transparent secure-field handling for create_return_id and the grouped store_sensitive_* acceptance tests do not cover it. Because Store + Sensitive callers can still invoke this method on the plaintext type, the feature does not fully uphold the promised single consistent caller-facing contract for Store APIs; the method should be hidden/blocked for sensitive models or explicitly implemented and covered.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared architecture notes already say create_return_id is out of scope for this milestone, but the feature leaves the method publicly reachable through Crud for all Store models and the transcript/handoff do not call out this remaining API gap.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md excludes transparent secure-field handling for create_return_id; core/src/repository/mod.rs:740-741 exposes Crud::create_return_id via Repo::<Self>::create_return_id(self.clone()).await; core/tests/integration_db.rs has no store_sensitive_* coverage for that method.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker ran the repository init script even though mission guidance says services/commands should come from .factory/services.yaml, and that file contains no init command.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows Execute pwsh -NoLogo -Command invoking C:\Users\admin\appdb\.factory\init.sh; .factory/services.yaml only defines Cargo commands and no init step.

summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, commit 4ada3aa0fed5fd65f3cd1d9e4d0ca3a184a19c69, relevant repo files, and mission shared-state docs. The transparent CRUD storage seam and grouped store_sensitive_* coverage are present, but the review fails because create_return_id remains publicly exposed to Store + Sensitive callers without a sensitive-model guard or explicit acceptance coverage.
